message:
feat(net): add Windows named-pipe client; fix(time): correct timer wheel coalescing group counting

Add a Windows named-pipe client surface (NamedPipeClientOptions,
NamedPipeClient) under src/net/sys/windows.rs, mirroring the
tokio::net::windows::named_pipe migration shape. The implementation
validates pipe-namespace paths, supports configurable read/write access,
and includes unit tests for path validation and access-mode enforcement.
Wire it into the net module's public API behind cfg(target_os="windows").

Fix a bug in TimerWheel::coalescing_group_size() where only timers in
the ready queue were counted, ignoring timers already placed in wheel
levels and the overflow list. The fix scans all occupied slots within
the coalescing window boundary across every level plus overflow entries.
Add coalescing_bug_test.rs as a targeted regression test.

Add wheel_cascade_test.rs to verify that timers placed at exact level
boundaries (e.g., tick 256 at level 0's boundary) correctly cascade
from higher levels and fire on time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
